data.title: Nasdaq futures rise as Tesla, Meta earnings cheer investors
data.description:
Stock index futures pointed higher on Thursday as investors digested a wave of tech earnings.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.42% to 7,007.06. Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.60% to 26,177.93, while Dow futures (INDU) +0.27% to 49,149.63.

Tesla (TSLA) shares were up 2.9% in premarket trade as a top- and bottom-line beat was coupled with an upbeat outlook on Cybercab and robotaxi growth. Meta Platforms (META) gained more than 7% after posting its fourth-quarter earnings, where it beat Wall Street expectations for revenues and profitability.

Microsoft (MSFT) shares fell more than 5% even as the tech giant reported fiscal second-quarter results that topped estimates.

Meanwhile, the Federal Reserve also kept its policy rate unchanged at 3.50%-3.75%

Treasury yields edged higher across the curve. The U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) ticked up to 3.58%, adding 0.17%. The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved higher to 4.27%, rising 0.45%. The 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) increased to 4.88%, up 0.70%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Meta Platforms (META) +7.96%, International Business Machines (IBM) +7.87%, and C.H. Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) +6.19%. Decliners included Las Vegas Sands (LVS) -9.89%, Microsoft (MSFT) -5.77%, and ServiceNow (NOW) -5.28%.

data.title: NEC gives nine-months results
data.description:
• NEC press release (NIPNF): Non-GAAP EPS for nine-months ending Dec 2025 of ¥107.29.
  • Revenue of ¥2.42T (+4.4% Y/Y).

data.title: Intrum AB (publ) reports FY results
data.description:
• Intrum AB (publ) press release (ITJTY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of -SEK11.25.
  • Revenue of SEK17.03B (-5.5% Y/Y).

data.title: Paradox Interactive gives FY results
data.description:
• Paradox Interactive press release (PRXXF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of SEK1.19.
  • Revenue of SEK2.19B (-0.5% Y/Y).
  • Operating profit amounted to SEK 146M (SEK 721.4M), a decrease of 80%.
  • Profit after financial items amounted to SEK 167.4M (SEK 757.3M), and profit after tax amounted to SEK 125.5M (SEK 584.6M).

data.title: Chugai Pharmaceutical reports FY results
data.description:
• Chugai Pharmaceutical press release (CHGCF): FY core operating profit and core net income for the fiscal year 2025 at ¥623.2 billion (+12.1%) and ¥451.0 billion (+13.6%) respectively, with a high core operating profit margin of 49.5% (all changes year-on-year [YoY])
  • Revenue of ¥1.26T (+7.7% Y/Y).
  • In 2026, core revenue, core operating profit, and core net income are expected to be ¥1,345.0 billion (+ ¥87.1 billion, +6.9%, YoY), ¥670.0 billion (+ ¥46.8 billion, +7.5%, YoY), and ¥485.0 billion (+ ¥34.0 billion, +7.5%, YoY), resulting in an increase in both revenue and profit.

data.title: TAL Education Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.25 beats by $0.17, revenue of $770.2M beats by $4.17M
data.description:
• TAL Education press release (TAL): Q3 Non-GAAP EPADS of $0.25 beats by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $770.17M (+27.0% Y/Y) beats by $4.17M.
  • Cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments totaled US$3,617.4 million as of November 30, 2025, compared to US$3,618.4 million as of February 28, 2025.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities for the third quarter of fiscal year 2026 was US$526.7 million.
  • As of November 30, 2025, the Company's deferred revenue balance was US$1,162.8 million, compared to US$671.2 million as of February 28, 2025.

data.title: Telia Company AB (publ) reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Telia Company AB (publ) press release (TLSNF): Q4 EPS decreased to SEK -0.33 (-0.13).
  • Total net income decreased to SEK -1.1 billion (-0.3)
  • Revenue amounted to SEK 21.3 billion (21.4) and increased 1.6%, like for like.
  • Service revenue increased 2.1%, like for like, driven by continued strong momentum in Sweden and the Baltics.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased 3.7%, like for like, driven by service revenue growth and lower operational expenses across most markets.

data.title: Sanofi anticipates profitable growth to continue over at least five years
data.description:
Sanofi (SNY) shares traded slightly lower in Paris on Thursday even as the French drugmaker reported higher-than-expected quarterly profit, thanks mainly to its asthma drug Dupixent, which is marketed in collaboration with Regeneron (REGN).

The Paris-based firm posted Q4 adjusted EPS of €1.53 on revenue of €11.3B (+7.0% Y/Y; 13.3% at constant exchange rates), both topping Street estimates. Dupixent sales rose by 32.2% to €4.2B, but vaccine sales fell 2.5% to €2B.

CEO Paul Hudson said, "In the fourth quarter, sales growth accelerated to 13.3%, delivering another strong performance. Growth was supported by new medicines and Dupixent, reaching a new quarterly high."

“In 2026, we expect sales to grow by a high single-digit percentage and business EPS to grow slightly faster than sales. We anticipate profitable growth to continue over at least five years.” The outlook for the year is largely in line with Bloomberg Intelligence expectations.

Vaccine sales “might be slightly negative” in 2026, but it’s too early to say, Francois-Xavier Roger, Sanofi’s chief financial officer, said on a call with journalists.

In addition, Sanofi also plans to execute a share buyback program in 2026 of €1B.

data.title: Wizz Air Holdings reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Wizz Air Holdings press release (WZZAF): Q3 Net loss of 139.3M vs. €241.1M loss in the same quarter last year.
  • Revenue of €1.3B (+10.2% Y/Y).
  • Wizz Air carried 17.5 million passengers, a 12.5 per cent increase compared to the same period in the previous year and generated revenues of €1,296.4 million, 10.2 per cent higher year-on-year.
  • ASK grew by 11.1 per cent year on year, while seats increased by 13.1%, reflecting the shorter stage length flown.
  • The load factor decreased by 0.5ppt to 89.8%.

data.title: Givaudan SA gives FY results
data.description:
• Givaudan SA press release (GVDBF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of CHF116.08.
  • Revenue of CHF7.5B,  an increase of 5.1% on a like-for-like  basis and an increase of 0.8% in Swiss francs.
  • High-growth markets growing at 8.0% and mature markets at 2.4% on a like-for-like basis.
  • EBITDA  of CHF 1,751 million, an increase of 4.5% over 2024, when measured in local currency.
  • EBITDA margin of 23.4%.
  • Comparable EBITDA  margin of 24.2% compared to 24.5% in 2024.
  • Net income of CHF 1,071 million; net profit margin of 14.3% of sales.
  • The Company is targeting 4–6% average like-for-like sales growth and over 12% average free cash flow over the five-year period and its 2030 purpose goals in the areas of nature, people and communities.
